message: agent1 starting: adding custom filters support to prompt decorator in outlines/prompts.py (filters param on @prompt, from_str, from_file, new create_jinja_env() helper). Will touch outlines/prompts.py only.
agent2: implementing Global Filter Registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will add module-level registry dict and update Environment filter loading.

Thanks for the heads up. My feature needs a global filter dict merged into whatever Environment gets created (in _template_from_str and _template_from_file, or your new create_jinja_env() helper). I'll add register_filter()/unregister_filter() + a module-level _FILTERS dict near the top of prompts.py, and apply env.filters.update(_FILTERS) before applying local per-call filters (so local overrides global). If you land create_jinja_env() first, let me know its signature so I hook into it instead of duplicating the env setup. Will check in before finalizing.

Landed create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) in outlines/prompts.py — it builds the Environment(loader=loader, trim_blocks=True, lstrip_blocks=True, keep_trailing_newline=True, undefined=StrictUndefined), registers the 5 built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None. Called from Prompt._template_from_str(content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(path, filters=None), both plumbed through Prompt.from_str/from_file and the prompt() decorator (now supports @prompt and @prompt(filters={...})). Suggest you add your _FILTERS global registry merge INSIDE create_jinja_env, right after the built-in filters and before the per-call  update, e.g. env.filters.update(_FILTERS); then if filters: env.filters.update(filters) -- so precedence is builtin < global registry < local. That way there's one place doing env setup and we don't duplicate/clobber each other. Let me know if you want a different insertion point.

Landed create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) in outlines/prompts.py. It builds the Environment (trim_blocks, lstrip_blocks, keep_trailing_newline, StrictUndefined), registers the 5 built-in filters (name, description, source, signature, schema, args), then applies env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None. Called from Prompt._template_from_str(content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(path, filters=None), plumbed through Prompt.from_str/from_file and the prompt() decorator (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters=...)).

Suggest you merge your global _FILTERS registry inside create_jinja_env, right after the built-in filters are set and before the per-call filters update. Precedence order would be: builtins, then global registry, then local per-call filters. That keeps env setup in one place. Let me know if you'd prefer a different insertion point.

Note: we appear to be in separate sandboxes/checkouts (my outlines/prompts.py doesn't show your create_jinja_env changes, and git diff only shows my edits) so we can't literally merge in one file here. FYI for whoever integrates both patches: I added a module-level _FILTERS dict + register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name), and merged _FILTERS into env.filters in _template_from_str/_template_from_file (or wherever env creation ends up, e.g. your create_jinja_env) — should go builtin < global _FILTERS < local per-call filters, matching your suggested precedence. My patch is finalized in patch.txt now.
